close_reason: Fixed: structured matching on Tss2ResponseCodeKind instead of string matching
description: map_tpm_error classifies errors by checking lower.contains() for keywords like 'retry', 'yielded', 'resource', 'tcti', and 'i/o error'. However, tss_esapi::Error Display output never contains these keywords. Tss2ResponseCode::Display for Retry prints 'the TPM was not able to start the command' (no 'retry'). Yielded prints 'the TPM has suspended operation on the command; forward progress was made and the command may be retried' (no 'yielded', though 'retry' substring-matches 'retried'). 'resource' appears in zero error Display strings. NvRate ('rate-limiting') and NvUnavailable ('NV is not current accessible') are transient warnings that should map to Unavailable but will fall through to Backend. The only accidental match is 'retry' catching Yielded via substring. All other transient TPM errors (Retry, NvRate, NvUnavailable, ContextGap, Memory, etc.) are misclassified as permanent Backend errors. Fix: match on the structured Error enum (Error::Tss2Error then .kind()) instead of string-matching.
title: map_tpm_error string-matching is non-functional
